title: Flipkart Microservices Architecture
summary: Authorized security assessment of Flipkart's HackerOne Vulnerability Disclosure Program focusing on authentication services, API gateways and supporting microservices. The engagement identified a confirmed High severity CORS misconfiguration alongside several defense-in-depth weaknesses affecting authentication, input validation and legacy infrastructure.

timeline.summary: Mapped the microservices architecture and enumerated public assets.
Validated authentication, JWT handling, CORS behaviour and service configurations.
Demonstrated an end-to-end exploitation path leveraging permissive CORS to access authenticated user data.
Compiled findings and submitted them through Flipkart's HackerOne program.

confidentiality.summary: Authenticated user information, including cart contents, addresses and access tokens, could potentially be read cross-origin.

integrity.summary: If complementary protections were absent, authenticated state-changing actions could potentially be performed.

availability.summary: No meaningful denial-of-service impact was identified.

privacy.summary: Exposure of authenticated customer information presents significant privacy implications.

regulatory.summary: Compromise of customer information could create compliance obligations depending on affected jurisdictions.

operational.summary: Weak centralized CORS policy affected multiple production services, increasing systemic risk.

lessons.summary: Centralized authentication services require equally centralized security policy enforcement.
Origin reflection combined with credentialed requests can undermine otherwise robust authentication mechanisms.
Strong JWT validation and CSRF protections significantly reduced the impact of several investigated attack paths.
Outdated services increase organizational attack surface even when partially isolated.

seo.description: Case study covering an authorized security assessment of Flipkart's microservices architecture with emphasis on authentication, API security and a confirmed CORS misconfiguration.
